source_jobs.title: Senior Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Our Company
At Quantum, we connect global brands with high-intent consumers. We turn intent into action and complexity into clarity.



Our Mission is clear, to help smarter, faster buying decisions. Our vision, to be the world’s most used digital comparison service, delivering value and simplicity to millions.



We drive growth through data-driven affiliate marketing built for performance from day one. Every campaign is measurable, optimised in real time, and designed to scale.



With in-house tech, full-stack analytics, and a compliance-first mindset, we give our partners an edge. We drive confident decisions and growth in regulated and high-growth sectors. We’ve helped ambitious brands grow faster, smarter, and globally.



The Role

Our Technology team is growing and we'd love you to grow with us. We're looking for a skilled and experienced Senior Data Engineer to help design and build a robust, scalable, and high-performance real-time data backbone.

In this role, you'll take a leading hand in the design, development, and implementation of a real-time data backbone. Your focus will be on building and maintaining scalable, high-performance data pipelines that support real-time data processing and analytics. You'll work closely with teams across the business to ensure our infrastructure meets growing data needs, leveraging modern technologies for efficient data ingestion, storage, and analysis.



Key Responsibilities

 - Design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines and infrastructure that power real-time analytics and reporting.

 - Take ownership of our semantic layer using Cube.js, enabling reliable, self-serve analytics across the organisation.

 - Integrate and unify data from Salesforce, CDP, and SCV sources, ensuring clean, consistent data flow across marketing and customer data platforms.

 - Lead the development of transformation workflows using dbt, with a strong emphasis on testing, documentation, and data quality.

 - Architect and manage pipeline orchestration via Apache Airflow, ensuring reliability and observability at every stage.

 - Monitor and optimise pipeline performance for low-latency, high-throughput use cases.

 - Collaborate closely with Data Architect, marketing analysts, and business stakeholders to ensure data infrastructure directly supports business objectives.

 - Mentor and support more junior engineers, helping to raise the bar across the team.



About You

 - Significant hands-on experience in data engineering, with a proven track record of leading and delivering complex data initiatives end to end.

 - Great understanding of semantic layers such as Cube.js you've built and maintained semantic layers and understand pre-aggregations, caching strategies, and multi-tenancy.

 - Strong experience integrating Salesforce Marketing Cloud, CDP, and Single Customer View (SCV) data you understand the nuances of customer and marketing data at scale.

 - Expert-level SQL and proven experience building robust transformation pipelines with dbt.

 - Solid experience orchestrating workflows with Apache Airflow in production environments.

 - Comfortable working across cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) for data ingestion, storage, and processing.

 - Proficiency in Python or a similar language for pipeline development and automation.

 - Strong data modelling instincts you think carefully about how data is structured, named, and consumed downstream.

 - You're as comfortable whiteboarding architecture with engineers as you are explaining data concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

 - Calm and focused in a fast-moving environment you can prioritise well and bring others along with you.

 - Excellent written and verbal English communication skills.

 - You lead by example, care about the team's success, and leave things better than you found them.



What You’ll Get

 - Private Health Care (Bupa)

 - Hybrid Working (3 days in office)

 - Travel Insurance

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Compa

source_jobs.title: Senior AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Our Company
At Quantum, we connect global brands with high-intent consumers. We turn intent into action and complexity into clarity.



Our Mission is clear, to help smarter, faster buying decisions. Our vision, to be the world’s most used digital comparison service, delivering value and simplicity to millions.



We drive growth through data-driven affiliate marketing built for performance from day one. Every campaign is measurable, optimised in real time, and designed to scale.



With in-house tech, full-stack analytics, and a compliance-first mindset, we give our partners an edge. We drive confident decisions and growth in regulated and high-growth sectors. We’ve helped ambitious brands grow faster, smarter, and globally.



The Role
We're looking for an experienced Senior AI Engineer to join our team.


You'll be at the forefront of designing, developing, and deploying AI systems that power personalised advertising, customer journey analytics, audience segmentation, and real-time bidding strategies.



Working closely with BI, data engineers, and product teams, you'll help build scalable AI solutions that deliver measurable business outcomes for some of the world's most recognised brands. Our infrastructure is powered by Google Cloud Platform (GCP), and we take pride in applying cutting-edge AI techniques to real-world marketing and advertising challenges.


Key Responsibilities

 - Architecture & Multi-Agent Design: Design, build, and deploy scalable multi-agent systems and generative AI features for Mar Tech challenges including ad targeting, user segmentation, and content personalisation.

 - Advanced Agentic Workflows: Research and implement agentic design patterns such as planning, reflection, and tool usage using orchestration frameworks like LangGraph, AutoGen, or CrewAI.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely across the organisation to translate ambiguous business objectives into discrete agent personas, tool definitions, and behavioural boundaries.

 - Performance & Guardrails: Monitor and optimise multi-agent systems in production, tracking agent-specific metrics including token efficiency, execution loops, and tool-calling accuracy to ensure system reliability.

 - Security & Compliance: Maintain high standards for data security, model explainability, and mitigation of agent-specific risks such as prompt injection and unintended tool execution in compliance with GDPR and CCPA.

 - Mentorship & Best Practices: Support and develop junior engineers and help champion best practices in agent-oriented programming, prompt engineering, model evaluation, and scalable cloud infrastructure.



About You

 - Experienced in software engineering, ideally in Ad Tech, Mar Tech, or similarly complex and high-scale environments

 - Experience building multi-agent or single-agent architecture with integrations to APIs and/or MCPs

 - A good understanding of supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning methods alongside experience working with foundation models and prompt engineering

 - Practical experience with GCP services or AWS equivalent such as Vertex AI, BigQuery ML, Dataflow, AI Platform Pipelines, and Dataproc

 - Practical experience with CI/CD for AI/ML, model versioning, monitoring, and AI Ops practices

 - An understanding of marketing and advertising concepts such as customer lifetime value, attribution modelling, real-time bidding, and audience targeting is a plus

 - Familiarity with data pipeline orchestration tools such as Apache Airflow and Kubernetes is a plus

 - You do your best work as part of a team and enjoy sharing knowledge just as much as gaining it

 - Confident navigating ambiguity and a fast moving environment

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Collaborative and grounded, you value the perspectives of others and create space for everyone to contribute



What You’ll Get

 - Private Health Care (Bupa)

 - Hybrid Working (3 days in office)

 - Travel Insurance

source_jobs.title: Sales Operations Executive
source_jobs.description:
Our Company
At Quantum, we connect global brands with high-intent consumers. We turn intent into action and complexity into clarity.



Our Mission is clear, to help smarter, faster buying decisions. Our vision, to be the world’s most used digital comparison service, delivering value and simplicity to millions.



We drive growth through data-driven affiliate marketing built for performance from day one. Every campaign is measurable, optimised in real time, and designed to scale.



With in-house tech, full-stack analytics, and a compliance-first mindset, we give our partners an edge. We drive confident decisions and growth in regulated and high-growth sectors. We’ve helped ambitious brands grow faster, smarter, and globally.



The role

Our Sales Operations team is growing! We’re looking for a well organised and detail-oriented Sales Operations Executive to join our team in London.



In this role you will keep our merchant data accurate, bring new merchants live, and make sure every deal and page is working as it should, with real ownership from day one and a clear view of how the whole commercial operation runs.



Key responsibilities

 - Own merchant records: keep merchant information accurate and up-to-date across our internal systems, reflecting any commercial change quickly and correctly.

 - Support merchant onboarding: bring new merchants onto the platform, manage the setup from start to finish, and check everything works before they go live.

 - Run quality checks: review merchant deals and pages regularly to confirm they display and function correctly, and flag anything that needs fixing.

 - Maintain content, pages and access: keep placements current and aligned to business needs, and keep shared account credentials organised so the right people have access at the right time.

 - Connect teams and improve processes: keep Commercial, BI and Operations aligned, and spot repetitive tasks worth automating to reduce day-to-day errors.



About you

 - Strong attention to detail and takes pride in keeping information accurate and well-organised.

 - Comfortable working with spreadsheets and picking up new tools and systems quickly.

 - A good communicator who is happy to collaborate with different teams and flag issues when they spot them.

 - Eager to learn and proactive in taking ownership of tasks without needing to be chased.

 - Methodical in your approach, you like to follow a process, spot inconsistencies, and see tasks through to completion.

 - You thrive when working as part of a team

 - Comfortable in a fast-paced environment

 - Have excellent written and verbal English skills

 - Last but not least, you’ll have no ego!



What you’ll Gain

 - Hands-on experience in sales operations and commercial processes.

 - A broad understanding of merchant management, from onboarding through to quality assurance.

 - Development in attention to detail, problem-solving, and cross-team communication.

 - Training and mentorship from experienced commercial and operations professionals







What You’ll Get

 - Private Health Care (Bupa)

 - Hybrid Working (3 days in office)

 - Travel Insurance

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Company Bonus Scheme

 - Paid for AI Subscription (Claude / ChatGPT / Gemini)

 - Market-Leading Training Programme

 - Recognition & Reward Scheme

 - Annual Company Conference

 - Regular Happy Hours & Team Lunches

 - Free Coffee, Drinks & Snacks
   
   

What’s the next step?

Our hiring process ensures we're recruiting the right people for the role. We ensure that people are as suitable for us as we are for them.

If you like the sound of what we're all about at Quantum and want to join a team where you can make an impact, please apply or contact us at careers@quantum.media.

source_jobs.title: Technical CRM Executive
source_jobs.description:
Our Company
At Quantum, we connect global brands with high-intent consumers. We turn intent into action and complexity into clarity.



Our Mission is clear, to help smarter, faster buying decisions. Our vision, to be the world’s most used digital comparison service, delivering value and simplicity to millions.



We drive growth through data-driven affiliate marketing built for performance from day one. Every campaign is measurable, optimised in real time, and designed to scale.



With in-house tech, full-stack analytics, and a compliance-first mindset, we give our partners an edge. We drive confident decisions and growth in regulated and high-growth sectors. We’ve helped ambitious brands grow faster, smarter, and globally.



The Role

Our CRM team is growing. We’re looking for a Technical CRM Executive to join our affiliate marketing business in London.



This is an opportunity for someone with some experience with either Adobe campaign or SFMC, as well as HTML, SQL queries, and JavaScript, to support the team in delivering a customer-centric, automated experience across email, SMS, and messaging channels.



In this role, you’ll be supporting the CRM Technical Manager as well as CRM team and its wider ecosystem, using tools such as SparkPost, Email on Acid, Adobe, Salesforce Marketing Cloud, SMS integrations, Adobe Campaign and Rebrandly.



You’ll work on the end-to-end delivery of CRM initiatives that rely on data and systems so you must have the technical aptitude and knowledge with JavaScript, APIs and ideally base understanding of SQL. This includes managing incoming Jira tickets, monitoring daily campaigns, supporting the wider team, maintaining data quality and compliance, managing automations and integrations, overseeing IP warm-ups, and help build new HTML modules.



If you’re hands-on, data-driven, and comfortable operating across multiple technologies, with the coding skills listed, we’d like to hear from you.



 

Key responsibilities

 - Internal SME of CRM-related systems

 - Responsible for all data and compliance activities across CRM systems

 - Supporting the CRM team with troubleshooting, training, and complex journey builds

 - Managing new IP warm-ups

 - QA for all data-related changes and system updates

 - Monitoring all customer interactions

 - Troubleshooting automation, journey, and HTML rendering issues

 - Developing new HTML modules

 - Work on launching SMS and potentially WhatsApp within SFMC

 - Working cross-functionally with Analytics, Product, and Technology teams to deliver effective, timely campaigns

 - Developing and managing campaign audiences

 
 

Preferred Skills, Qualifications and Experience:

 - Either Salesforce Marketing Cloud (SFMC) or Adobe Campaign working experience

 - Strong data skills, including SQL and data quality control

 - Good working knowledge of HTML for email

 - Strong working knowledge of JavaScript

 - Understanding and ideally exposure with APIs and working with JSON Objects

 - Experience managing multiple priorities and adapting to changing requirements at short notice in a fast-paced environment

 - Ability to work to tight deadlines, both independently and under pressure, to deliver results for stakeholders

 - Commercially minded, data-literate, and highly analytical, with the ability to use raw data to generate insights and inform decisions

 

Nice to haves

 - Previous experience in iGaming or a marketplace environment

 - Strong understanding of email deliverability best practices, including IP warm-up, Google Postmaster, SNDS, and domain configuration

 - Experience working across multiple brands, verticals, and locales

 - Experience with SMS and WhatsApp channels

 - Experience working in a target-driven, revenue-focused environment



What You’ll Get

 - Private Health Care (Bupa)

 - Hybrid Working (3 days in office)

 - Travel Insurance

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Company Bonus Scheme

 - Paid for AI Subscription (Claude / ChatGPT

source_jobs.title: Management Accountant
source_jobs.description:
Our Company
At Quantum, we connect global brands with high-intent consumers. We turn intent into action and complexity into clarity.



Our Mission is clear, to help smarter, faster buying decisions. Our vision, to be the world’s most used digital comparison service, delivering value and simplicity to millions.



We drive growth through data-driven affiliate marketing built for performance from day one. Every campaign is measurable, optimised in real time, and designed to scale.



With in-house tech, full-stack analytics, and a compliance-first mindset, we give our partners an edge. We drive confident decisions and growth in regulated and high-growth sectors. We’ve helped ambitious brands grow faster, smarter, and globally.



The Role

Our Finance team is growing. We're looking for a Management Accountant to join us in London, reporting to the Financial Controller.

We're entering a more complex chapter. A recent acquisition, growth across three legal entities and the departure of a finance business partner have stretched the team. This role brings the management accounting and analytical capacity to meet it.

You'll own recurring reporting and reconciliations end to end, turn month end numbers into insight the business acts on, and create room for the Financial Controller to focus on commercial and strategic priorities. Within a year you'll run core month end independently and be the person the wider business trusts for a straight answer on the numbers.



Key Responsibilities

 - Prepare management accounts and month end reporting across all Quantum entities

 - Own balance sheet reconciliations and assigned recurring reporting end to end

 - Investigate variances, find the root cause and resolve discrepancies, producing analysis the business can act on

 - Build and improve reporting, reconciliation and control processes, including automation that reduces manual effort and strengthens controls

 - Support budgeting, forecasting and cashflow, and contribute to strategic finance initiatives as you grow into the role

 - Act as a trusted point of contact for finance queries and analysis across finance and the wider business

 - Take clear ownership of core responsibilities to reduce key person dependency within the function



About You

 - Qualified (ACA, ACCA or CIMA) preferred. We will also consider candidates who are part qualified or qualified by experience with solid management accounting experience

 - Experience preparing management accounts, running month end and owning balance sheet reconciliations

 - Good analytical instincts you investigate variances, find the root cause and resolve discrepancies rather than just flagging them

 - Confident working with Excel to an advanced level including data analysis, modeling and reporting

 - Able to manage multiple priorities at pace without compromising on accuracy or attention to detail

 - You do your best work as part of a team and enjoy contributing to a collaborative environment

 - Comfortable navigating a fast paced and ever changing environment

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - A genuine desire to learn, grow, and lift others around you

 - Multi-entity accounting experience and exposure to budgeting, forecasting, cashflow and working capital

 - A background in digital marketing, technology, SaaS or a high growth environment

 - A track record of process improvement or finance automation

 - Exposure to project appraisal, business cases or investment analysis and software development capitalisation principles

 - Familiarity with Xero, Tableau, Google Workspace, G-Accon and AI tools such as Claude or ChatGPT



What You’ll Get

 - Private Health Care (Bupa)

 - Hybrid Working (3 days in office)

 - Travel Insurance

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Company Bonus Scheme

 - Paid for AI Subscription (Claude / ChatGPT / Gemini)

 - Market-Leading Training Programme

 - Recognition & Reward Scheme

 - Annual Company Conference

 - Regular Happy

source_jobs.title: Financial Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Our Company
At Quantum, we connect global brands with high-intent consumers. We turn intent into action and complexity into clarity.



Our Mission is clear, to help smarter, faster buying decisions. Our vision, to be the world’s most used digital comparison service, delivering value and simplicity to millions.



We drive growth through data-driven affiliate marketing built for performance from day one. Every campaign is measurable, optimised in real time, and designed to scale.



With in-house tech, full-stack analytics, and a compliance-first mindset, we give our partners an edge. We drive confident decisions and growth in regulated and high-growth sectors. We’ve helped ambitious brands grow faster, smarter, and globally.



The Role

Our Finance team is growing. We're looking for a Finance Analyst to join our team in London, reporting to the Financial Controller and working closely with the Management Accountant and the Accounts Receivable and Payable teams.

Following a recent acquisition and continued growth across multiple legal entities, this role adds operational and reporting capacity to keep the function running smoothly. In this role you will own member payment processing and assigned reconciliations, support month end and recurring reporting, and flex across finance operations to keep processes accurate, efficient and scalable. Within a year you will run your reconciliations, payment runs and reporting independently, deliver process improvements, and be a dependable, multi-disciplinary member of the team.

 

Key Responsibilities

 - Process member payment runs accurately and on schedule, within established financial controls

 - Own assigned bank, balance sheet and transactional reconciliations across all entities

 - Support month end activities and recurring reporting with growing independence

 - Provide reliable support to the Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable teams during busy periods

 - Investigate discrepancies and resolve them within established financial controls

 - Identify and implement improvements to reporting, controls and finance processes, including automation that reduces manual effort

 - Build effective working relationships across the Finance team and wider stakeholders

 

About You

 - Experience in a finance, accounting, finance analyst or assistant management accountant role

 - Good reconciliation skills across bank, balance sheet or transactional reconciliations

 - Experience processing financial transactions accurately within established financial controls

 - Confident working with financial data and comfortable using Excel to a good standard

 - A strong eye for detail with the ability to manage multiple priorities at pace

 - You do your best work as part of a team and enjoy contributing to a collaborative environment

 - Comfortable navigating a fast paced and ever changing environment

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - A genuine desire to learn, grow, and lift others around you

 - AAT qualified or studying towards ACA, ACCA or CIMA

 - Month end support or management accounts exposure

 - Member or customer payment processing experience

 - Experience working across multi-entity environments

 - Budgeting and forecasting exposure

 - Interest in or experience with finance automation

 - Familiarity with Xero, Tableau or AI tools such as Claude or ChatGPT



What You’ll Get

 - Private Health Care (Bupa)

 - Hybrid Working (3 days in office)

 - Travel Insurance

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Company Bonus Scheme

 - Paid for AI Subscription (Claude / ChatGPT / Gemini)

 - Market-Leading Training Programme

 - Recognition & Reward Scheme

 - Annual Company Conference

 - Regular Happy Hours & Team Lunches

 - Free Coffee, Drinks & Snacks
   
   

What’s the next step?

Our hiring process ensures we're recruiting the right people for the role. We ensure that people are as suitable for us as we are for them.

source_jobs.title: Head of Data Engineering and Machine Learning
source_jobs.description:
Our Company
At Quantum, we connect global brands with high-intent consumers. We turn intent into action and complexity into clarity.



Our Mission is clear, to help smarter, faster buying decisions. Our vision, to be the world’s most used digital comparison service, delivering value and simplicity to millions.



We drive growth through data-driven affiliate marketing built for performance from day one. Every campaign is measurable, optimised in real time, and designed to scale.



With in-house tech, full-stack analytics, and a compliance-first mindset, we give our partners an edge. We drive confident decisions and growth in regulated and high-growth sectors. We’ve helped ambitious brands grow faster, smarter, and globally.



The Role



Our Data and Machine Learning team is growing! We’re looking for a Head of Data Engineering and Machine Learning to join our team in London.

In this role you will lead the data and ML teams that power the Quantum Platform, own the technical strategy and delivery roadmap for our data infrastructure and model estate, and turn business ambition into intelligence that scales.



Key Responsibilities



 - Build, lead and develop a high-performing data engineering and ML team, recruiting and retaining engineers who take pride in what they ship and how it performs

 - Set and own the data engineering and ML technical strategy, so our data infrastructure and model estate scale with the business

 - Lead the delivery roadmap, meeting business objectives and making well-reasoned trade-offs where they are needed

 - Partner with product, marketing, commercial and executive leadership to shape data and ML priorities that move business performance

 - Set the technical bar and drive adoption of AI-led development across the function, staying close to the code where it counts



About You

 - Proven experience leading and developing data engineering or ML teams, setting direction and building capability

 - A track record of delivering data and ML platforms that drove real customer and business results

 - Hands-on technical credibility: you enjoy getting into the detail to help your team land the right solution

 - Able to translate technical complexity into clear, business-relevant narratives for stakeholders at every level

 - Strong commercial acumen, comfortable engaging senior stakeholders on revenue, growth and competitive advantage

 - You thrive when working as part of a team

 - Comfortable in a fast-paced environment

 - Have excellent written and verbal English skills

 - Last but not least, you’ll have no ego!



What You’ll Get

 - Private Health Care (Bupa)

 - Hybrid Working (3 days in office)

 - Travel Insurance

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Company Bonus Scheme

 - Paid for AI Subscription (Claude / ChatGPT / Gemini)

 - Market-Leading Training Programme

 - Recognition & Reward Scheme

 - Annual Company Conference

 - Regular Happy Hours & Team Lunches

 - Free Coffee, Drinks & Snacks
   
   

What’s the next step?

Our hiring process ensures we're recruiting the right people for the role. We ensure that people are as suitable for us as we are for them.

If you like the sound of what we're all about at Quantum and want to join a team where you can make an impact, please apply or contact us at careers@quantum.media.

notes: Data-driven affiliate marketing company with in-house tech and full-stack analytics
